reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — headless Chrome real-inference verification (fresh Download + cached auto-init passes) on the model page, wild cross-lingual page, and multi-model chain; multilingual correctness spot-checks; desktop/mobile screenshots; responsive matrix

rubric.evidence: Real headless run (WASM, no-GPU): fresh profile → download-required + Download (~340 MB) button → clicked → ready → auto-classify of the Spanish sample against 5 Spanish labels produced ranked scores. Readout: 'backend WASM latency 967 ms labels 5 mode single-label'. Console errors: 0; failed network requests: 0. Standalone verification also confirmed correct classes: German 'Angela Merkel …' → politics (0.40).
rubric.notes: MoritzLaurer/mDeBERTa-v3-base-mnli-xnli declares model_type 'deberta-v2' and loads on the existing zero-shot-classification pipeline in transformers.js 3.7.5 — NO version pin. Inference in models/mdeberta-zero-shot/worker.js (Web Worker).

rubric.evidence: Expected shape confirmed: pipeline returns {labels (sorted), scores} across the candidate labels; single-label mode softmaxes across labels, multi-label scores each independently. See-inside reads the raw 3-way NLI head — verified config.id2label={0:entailment,1:neutral,2:contradiction} so the entailment slice is index 0. Cross-lingual spot-check (English labels {technology,sports,politics,food}) classified Spanish→technology, French→sports, German→politics, Japanese→food, Russian→politics, Portuguese→sports, all correct.
rubric.notes: q8 base scores are modest in absolute terms and the model is not infallible on fine-grained/overlapping label sets — the demos use small, distinct label sets and state the limitation honestly.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance shows the exact source id MoritzLaurer/mDeBERTa-v3-base-mnli-xnli linked to its card (and the microsoft/mdeberta-v3-base origin), task zero-shot-classification, model_type deberta-v2, languages (100 pretrain / MNLI+XNLI fine-tune), quantisation q8, backend WebAssembly, download ~340 MB, license MIT. Loader detail confirms 'MoritzLaurer/mDeBERTa-v3-base-mnli-xnli · transformers.js · ~340 MB'.
rubric.notes: Honest disclosure of the delta vs the built English DeBERTa/BART-MNLI zero-shot demos (multilingual encoder) with direct cross-links — no invented API.

rubric.evidence: Two-pass headless test on a persistent profile (model page + wild page). PASS 1 (fresh): loader 'download-required' + Download (~340 MB) button — no silent transfer. PASS 2 (cached reload): loader auto-inits to 'ready', NO Download button present — auto-init from cache works per the shared createModelLoader contract.
rubric.notes: shared-auto-init-loader = pass; per-model 'Clear cached model' control present (visible in the wild-page screenshot); ~340 MB honestly disclosed.

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live announces scoring/done/error; a .err status surfaces failures. Verified download-required, ready, and 'Done.' states live across the model, wild, and multi-model pages. The shared loader provides retry/recovery.
rubric.notes: Offline-cached and eviction paths handled by the shared loader but not separately driven this pass.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls verified live: six language sample chips (keyboard-activatable), a text textarea (dir=auto), a labels input, an editable hypothesis-template input, a multi-label checkbox, and Classify. Practical page adds a routing/moderation radio group + inbox; wild adds 'Classify all languages' + a free-text box; multi-model adds sample chips + two loaders. All keyboard-operable.
rubric.notes: A full driven touch traversal at 360px (chips, template input, multi-label toggle) is the remaining check before flipping mobile to ok.

rubric.evidence: Fresh profile shows the honest absent-model state and empty score/see-inside regions ('Classify some text above to inspect the entailment logits.'); bars and the entailment breakdown appear ONLY after a real on-device run. All scores are the model's real output; the see-inside shows the actual hypothesis sentence and the raw 3-way softmax.
rubric.notes: The wild page presents the real cross-lingual results with real confidences (e.g. Spanish→technology 39%) rather than rescaling to look confident.

rubric.evidence: buttons-have-names holds (sample chips carry aria-label, all buttons have text); no content images (images-have-alt vacuously true). Status region is role=status aria-live; score bars and NLI stacks are labelled; text fields use dir=auto so RTL (Arabic/Hebrew) and CJK render correctly. Verified in headless DOM checks.
rubric.notes: dir=auto on the text/label fields + result labels is important for a multilingual demo (Arabic/Hindi/Japanese render correctly).

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — scrollWidth<=innerWidth, zero clipped controls, console+network clean. The At-a-glance table sits in .matrix-scroll (overflow-x:auto); the wild/multi-model two-column layouts use auto-fit minmax(min(280px,100%),1fr); long labels wrap via overflow-wrap:anywhere.
rubric.notes: Desktop + mobile kept needs-review pending a driven touch matrix pass (automated signal only, per the harness rule).

rubric.evidence: All inference runs in models/mdeberta-zero-shot/worker.js (off the main thread); the page stays responsive during the ~340 MB load and multi-label re-runs. The wild 'classify all' loops six messages sequentially in the worker (3637 ms total for 6) with a live per-message status, so the main thread never blocks.
rubric.notes: An explicit INP trace while scoring a long pasted document would confirm the tokenize+forward path stays fully off-thread.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel layout without horizontal overflow, multilingual text
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing, auto-fit grid tracks minmax(min(280px,100%),1fr), wrap wide tables in overflow-x:auto, and let long unbreakable label tokens wrap (overflow-wrap:anywhere) so nothing forces page overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the two-column wild/multi-model layouts use .grid-2 auto-fit grids; the At-a-glance table sits in .matrix-scroll; label/score rows use overflow-wrap:anywhere and white-space:nowrap only on the numeric score. Re-verified desktop+mobile scrollWidth<=innerWidth.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status, keyboard-operable controls, bidirectional/multilingual text
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state in a role=status aria-live region; give every control an accessible name; use dir=auto on user-editable multilingual text so RTL/CJK render correctly.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared createModelLoader status is role=status aria-live; sample chips carry aria-label; text/label inputs and result labels use dir=auto so Arabic (RTL), Hindi, Japanese, and Chinese render correctly. buttons-have-names assertion passes.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headless DOM checks (buttons-have-names); live-status-region assertion; dir=auto on fields

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low, run heavy multilingual inference off the main thread
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy computation off the main thread; report incremental progress for batch work.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all NLI inference runs in a Web Worker; the practical/wild batch loops report a live per-message status so the UI stays responsive across the six sequential classifications.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: inference-off-main-thread source assertion; wild batch 6 messages in 3637 ms with live status

guidanceConsulted.query: catalogue to model page navigation transitions
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css (invariant 6).

openQuestions: Drive a full touch-interaction pass at 360×740 (sample chips, template input, multi-label toggle, routing/moderation radios) before flipping mobile support to ok.

q8 mDeBERTa-base cross-lingual accuracy drops on fine-grained/overlapping label sets (e.g. travel vs sports, health vs food) and short text; a fp16 build would likely help but roughly triples the download — worth offering an optional higher-precision text tower?

The default hypothesis template is English ('This example is {}.'); the XNLI literature suggests a language-matched template can help for some languages — worth surfacing per-language templates as a teachable control?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for mdeberta-zero-shot: drive the sample chips, template input, multi-label toggle, and practical routing/moderation radios on mobile with touch, verify tap targets/focus, then flip mobile support to ok.

Add a 'confusion explorer' wild demo: run the SAME text against several candidate label sets (coarse vs fine-grained) to show where cross-lingual zero-shot is reliable and where it gets shaky — teaching the honest limits of the technique.
Add a per-language hypothesis-template control and measure whether a language-matched template improves accuracy on the XNLI languages, exposing the template's effect in the see-inside view.

summary: mdeberta-zero-shot is a strong, honest demo of MoritzLaurer's mDeBERTa-v3-base-mnli-xnli, distinct from the built English DeBERTa/BART-MNLI zero-shot demos by its multilingual encoder: real zero-shot-classification via natural-language inference in ~100 languages (loads on transformers.js 3.7.5 with no version pin, model_type deberta-v2) in a Web Worker, verified end-to-end in headless Chrome — fresh Download (~340 MB) → real ranked scores, cached reload → auto-init no button, console clean. The see-inside shows the real 3-way entailment logits and the exact (any-language) hypothesis sentence. The ladder is genuinely multilingual: basics (classify native-language samples), practical (route/moderate a mixed-language inbox), wild (ONE English label set correctly classifying Spanish/French/German/Japanese/Russian/Portuguese text — all six verified correct), and multi-model (XLM-RoBERTa language-detect → mDeBERTa classify). Fields use dir=auto so RTL/CJK render correctly. Desktop+mobile responsive-check automated-clean (kept needs-review pending a driven touch pass). The q8 base's cross-lingual limits on fine-grained labels are stated honestly. Revision 1.
